title: Allophylus puberulus
taxonomy: Allophylus puberulus belongs to the family Sapindaceae, which is a family of flowering plants commonly known as the soapberry or soapbark family. This family comprises around 140 genera and 1,400 species. Allophylus puberulus is a part of the genus Allophylus, a group of flowering plants native to tropical regions of Africa and Asia. The genus Allophylus is characterized by its woody plants with compound leaves and small flowers. Allophylus puberulus is a species of flowering plant that falls under the kingdom Plantae.

intro: Allophylus puberulus is a plant species in the family Sapindaceae, commonly known as the rough-leaved allophyllus. Traditionally, Allophylus puberulus has been used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, inflammation, and gastrointestinal issues, due to its antipyretic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Allophylus puberulus include flavonoids, terpenoids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal activities. Allophylus puberulus is commonly prepared as a decoction or infusion, and is also used to make herbal teas and tonics, which are believed to promote overall health and well-being.
